# Text Elements
How function calls works in languages ^70Y96MPr

-> Now main function is always in stack
memory until the execution of all operation
is completed.

-> if a function is calling another function,
the function calling the other function 
stays in the stack.... This can work as a 
hierarchy, with main function being the root 
node of the whole hierarchy.
  ^ISxifkxI

# While the function is not finished
executing it remains in stack ^dI6iCisV

# When a function finished executing 
it is removed from the stack and 
returns the flow of program
is restored to where the function was 
called ^P3jKJ9sp

What is Recursion ^ChsdGppt

# Recursion is nothing but a condition 
in which a function calls itself... ^mKAysLFV

for stopping the function from

CODE Example : 
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        print(1);
    }    

    static void print(int n){
        if(n == 5){
            System.out.println(n);
            return;
        }
        System.out.println(n);
        print(n+1);
    } ^btlP4EgF

you can treat it as a separate call in the stack ^QFwuJA2d

happening, stack will be filled again and again 
and each function consumes some memory --->
 ^jEBkCGx3

# Resulting in Stack over flow error,
i.e. Memory limit of computer is exceeded. ^DhJgnLdf

    static int search(int[] arr, int target, int s, int e){
        if (s > e){
            return -1;
        }
        int m = s + (e-s)/2;

        if(arr[m] == target){
            return m;
        }
        if(target < arr[m]){
            search(arr, target, s, m-1);
        }
        return search(arr, target, m+1, e);
    } ^vnNOtLsk

Permutations ^PwMtaOAo

10 ^cZKa7M7X

Permutation is a mathematical term 
for the number of ways a group of 
objects can be assembled into a set. 
It's similar to another mathematical 
term, combination , except for one key 
difference: with permutations, the order 
of the itmes in the set makes a difference. ^5HPxjYTS

Formula : Pr = n!/(n – r)! ^shviezkY

11 ^3DhC2vsi

Backtracking is a general algorithmic technique that is often 
used in data structures to solve problems recursively by trying 
out various possible solutions and then undoing them if they 
do not lead to a solution. ^s5xJtQS7

		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 1251,
			"versionNonce": 759564117,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "mKAysLFV",
			"fillStyle": "solid",
			"strokeWidth": 1,
			"strokeStyle": "solid",
			"roughness": 1,
			"opacity": 100,
			"angle": 0,
			"x": -661.2275002199679,
			"y": 329.42049198419346,
			"strokeColor": "#2f9e44",
			"backgroundColor": "transparent",
			"width": 308.12786865234375,
			"height": 40,
			"seed": 542940456,
			"groupIds": [],
			"frameId": null,
			"roundness": null,
			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525596,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 16,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "# Recursion is nothing but a condition \nin which a function calls itself...",
			"rawText": "# Recursion is nothing but a condition \nin which a function calls itself...",
			"textAlign": "center",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "# Recursion is nothing but a condition \nin which a function calls itself...",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},
		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 1501,
			"versionNonce": 931169403,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "TcZyPgAO",
			"fillStyle": "solid",
			"strokeWidth": 1,
			"strokeStyle": "solid",
			"roughness": 1,
			"opacity": 100,
			"angle": 0,
			"x": -663.0110841192176,
			"y": 389.27848700264104,
			"strokeColor": "#2f9e44",
			"backgroundColor": "transparent",
			"width": 302.19183349609375,
			"height": 100,
			"seed": 1168966440,
			"groupIds": [],
			"frameId": null,
			"roundness": null,
			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525596,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 16,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "# In recursion their must be a check \ncondition or base condition \nfor stopping the function from \ncalling itself when the \nrequirement is completed.  ",
			"rawText": "# In recursion their must be a check \ncondition or base condition \nfor stopping the function from \ncalling itself when the \nrequirement is completed.  ",
			"textAlign": "center",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "# In recursion their must be a check \ncondition or base condition \nfor stopping the function from \ncalling itself when the \nrequirement is completed.  ",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},
		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 2024,
			"versionNonce": 1645499573,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "btlP4EgF",
			"fillStyle": "solid",
			"strokeWidth": 1,
			"strokeStyle": "solid",
			"roughness": 1,
			"opacity": 100,
			"angle": 0,
			"x": -674.8889431759266,
			"y": 504.4153222846977,
			"strokeColor": "#1971c2",
			"backgroundColor": "transparent",
			"width": 309.70941162109375,
			"height": 237.35526243962354,
			"seed": 1714832424,
			"groupIds": [],
			"frameId": null,
			"roundness": null,
			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525596,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 14.606477688592218,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "CODE Example : \n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        print(1);\n    }    \n\n    static void print(int n){\n        if(n == 5){\n            System.out.println(n);\n            return;\n        }\n        System.out.println(n);\n        print(n+1);\n    }",
			"rawText": "CODE Example : \n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        print(1);\n    }    \n\n    static void print(int n){\n        if(n == 5){\n            System.out.println(n);\n            return;\n        }\n        System.out.println(n);\n        print(n+1);\n    }",
			"textAlign": "left",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "CODE Example : \n    public static void main(String[] args) {\n        print(1);\n    }    \n\n    static void print(int n){\n        if(n == 5){\n            System.out.println(n);\n            return;\n        }\n        System.out.println(n);\n        print(n+1);\n    }",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},

		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 858,
			"versionNonce": 355600277,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "LU3lD7mv",

			"y": 634.1554475392062,

			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525600,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 16,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "(1) Linear recurrent relations : Fibonacci num\n(2) Divide and conquer : Binary search\n(Returned by a factor) ",
			"rawText": "(1) Linear recurrent relations : Fibonacci num\n(2) Divide and conquer : Binary search\n(Returned by a factor) ",
			"textAlign": "center",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "(1) Linear recurrent relations : Fibonacci num\n(2) Divide and conquer : Binary search\n(Returned by a factor) ",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},
		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 1222,
			"versionNonce": 344172603,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "vnNOtLsk",
			"fillStyle": "solid",
			"strokeWidth": 0.5,
			"strokeStyle": "dashed",
			"roughness": 2,
			"opacity": 100,
			"angle": 0,
			"x": 1487.619396959171,
			"y": 358.06024405800565,
			"strokeColor": "#099268",
			"backgroundColor": "transparent",
			"width": 384.7109375,
			"height": 240.7796827782052,
			"seed": 938914442,
			"groupIds": [],
			"frameId": null,
			"roundness": null,
			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525600,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 13.758839015897438,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "    static int search(int[] arr, int target, int s, int e){\n        if (s > e){\n            return -1;\n        }\n        int m = s + (e-s)/2;\n\n        if(arr[m] == target){\n            return m;\n        }\n        if(target < arr[m]){\n            search(arr, target, s, m-1);\n        }\n        return search(arr, target, m+1, e);\n    }",
			"rawText": "    static int search(int[] arr, int target, int s, int e){\n        if (s > e){\n            return -1;\n        }\n        int m = s + (e-s)/2;\n\n        if(arr[m] == target){\n            return m;\n        }\n        if(target < arr[m]){\n            search(arr, target, s, m-1);\n        }\n        return search(arr, target, m+1, e);\n    }",
			"textAlign": "left",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "    static int search(int[] arr, int target, int s, int e){\n        if (s > e){\n            return -1;\n        }\n        int m = s + (e-s)/2;\n\n        if(arr[m] == target){\n            return m;\n        }\n        if(target < arr[m]){\n            search(arr, target, s, m-1);\n        }\n        return search(arr, target, m+1, e);\n    }",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},

		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 2521,
			"versionNonce": 1780228923,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "5HPxjYTS",
			"fillStyle": "solid",
			"strokeWidth": 1,
			"strokeStyle": "solid",
			"roughness": 1,
			"opacity": 100,
			"angle": 0,
			"x": 2903.0987678651163,
			"y": 346.95669459792373,
			"strokeColor": "#2f9e44",
			"backgroundColor": "transparent",
			"width": 347.48779296875,
			"height": 140,
			"seed": 113208817,
			"groupIds": [],
			"frameId": null,
			"roundness": null,
			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525605,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 16,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "Permutation is a mathematical term \nfor the number of ways a group of \nobjects can be assembled into a set. \nIt's similar to another mathematical \nterm, combination , except for one key \ndifference: with permutations, the order \nof the itmes in the set makes a difference.",
			"rawText": "Permutation is a mathematical term \nfor the number of ways a group of \nobjects can be assembled into a set. \nIt's similar to another mathematical \nterm, combination , except for one key \ndifference: with permutations, the order \nof the itmes in the set makes a difference.",
			"textAlign": "center",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "Permutation is a mathematical term \nfor the number of ways a group of \nobjects can be assembled into a set. \nIt's similar to another mathematical \nterm, combination , except for one key \ndifference: with permutations, the order \nof the itmes in the set makes a difference.",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},

			"updated": 1718529525606,

			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 20,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "Point : In this case the number of recursive \ncalls inside the function is a single point incremental \nvariable,.... as the size of the recursive calls is \nincreasing by one as the height of recursive tree\nis increasing. ",
			"rawText": "Point : In this case the number of recursive \ncalls inside the function is a single point incremental \nvariable,.... as the size of the recursive calls is \nincreasing by one as the height of recursive tree\nis increasing. ",
			"textAlign": "center",

			"originalText": "Point : In this case the number of recursive \ncalls inside the function is a single point incremental \nvariable,.... as the size of the recursive calls is \nincreasing by one as the height of recursive tree\nis increasing. ",
			"lineHeight": 1.25

		{
			"type": "text",
			"version": 331,
			"versionNonce": 1876219867,
			"isDeleted": false,
			"id": "s5xJtQS7",
			"fillStyle": "solid",
			"strokeWidth": 0.5,
			"strokeStyle": "dashed",
			"roughness": 2,
			"opacity": 100,
			"angle": 0,
			"x": 3800.18555546051,
			"y": -265.28306150582387,
			"strokeColor": "#2f9e44",
			"backgroundColor": "transparent",
			"width": 636.6194458007812,
			"height": 100,
			"seed": 1026343519,
			"groupIds": [],
			"frameId": null,
			"roundness": null,
			"boundElements": [],
			"updated": 1718529525606,
			"link": null,
			"locked": false,
			"fontSize": 20,
			"fontFamily": 1,
			"text": "Backtracking is a general algorithmic technique that is often \nused in data structures to solve problems recursively by trying \nout various possible solutions and then undoing them if they \ndo not lead to a solution.",
			"rawText": "Backtracking is a general algorithmic technique that is often \nused in data structures to solve problems recursively by trying \nout various possible solutions and then undoing them if they \ndo not lead to a solution.",
			"textAlign": "center",
			"verticalAlign": "top",
			"containerId": null,
			"originalText": "Backtracking is a general algorithmic technique that is often \nused in data structures to solve problems recursively by trying \nout various possible solutions and then undoing them if they \ndo not lead to a solution.",
			"lineHeight": 1.25
		},
